title: Make lockfile reproducibility fail closed on compiler errors
outOfScope: Release publication, tags, provider assets, and adopter-project installation.
Unrelated release metadata or workflow behavior.

sources.reason: The lockfile reproducibility recipe currently can return success after a compiler failure.
Regression coverage must prove compiler failures propagate as non-zero.
Predecessor Work Item closure and release-candidate evidence.

riskAssessment.reason: A false-positive reproducibility gate can permit invalid dependency state into review or release.

executionDecision.reason: The failure mode, scope, acceptance, and verification are deterministic.

preReviewWarnings: Verify that both shell failure propagation and the existing byte-for-byte lockfile comparison remain fail closed.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: make check-lockfile-reproducibility exits non-zero when the lockfile compiler exits non-zero.
A regression test exercises the failing-compiler path without requiring piptools to be installed.
Successful compilation still requires the generated lockfile to match the committed lockfile byte-for-byte.
The Work Item Summary records the root cause, verification evidence, budget impact, and any environment limitation.

intent.problem: The lockfile reproducibility Make target can report success when pip-compile fails because the subshell failure is not propagated to the recipe exit status. This is a process-level false positive discovered during final release preflight.
constraints: Fix the shell error propagation at the Make target boundary.
Preserve the existing executable resolution and exact lockfile comparison behavior.
Do not publish or modify release metadata in this Work Item.
Do not create a budget-only Work Item; record the archive and source-line budget change here.
intent.rationale: A deterministic fail-closed gate prevents repeated CI and PR cycles caused by trusting a check that silently masks compiler errors.

rawUserRequest: When a release or CI process exposes a process problem, stop publication and fix the root workflow first. Budget and formatting must be hard preflight checks, and each Work Item must complete the full Contract/Preflight/PR/merge/ai-close-work-item lifecycle. Do not use a separate budget-only Work Item.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: A lockfile compiler executable fails; the Make target must fail.
scenarioCoverage.reason: Regression test for the discovered false-positive path.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Compilation succeeds but output differs; the Make target must fail.
Compilation succeeds and output matches; the Make target must pass.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User authorized root-cause process improvement before resuming release; the policy budget change is part of this functional Work Item, not a separate budget-only Work Item.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
